ocr.text: The table presents the increase over control (pg/mL) for various mediators. For G-CSF, the increase is 21.87 ± 4.57, indicating a
significant elevation. IL-6 shows a markedly higher increase at 267.81 ± 28.35, suggesting a robust inflammatory response. KC
has an increase of 356.38 ± 57.98, which is the highest among the mediators listed, reflecting substantial chemotactic activity.
MIP2 follows with an increase of 198.70 ± 12.24, indicative of its role in immune cell recruitment. MCP-1 demonstrates a
modest increase of 2.01 ± 1.54, showing its involvement in monocyte trafficking, while TNF-α has a notable increase of 33.16
± 4.55, which is critical in mediating the inflammatory process. IFN-γ presents a lower increase at 1.44 ± 0.88, suggesting a
limited role in this context. IL-1β shows an increase of 0.51 ± 0.35, reflecting its contribution to the inflammatory cascade but
at a lower magnitude. IL10, known for its anti-inflammatory properties, exhibits an increase of 0.95 ± 0.62, which may indicate
a compensatory mechanism in response to inflammation. Lastly, Eotaxin reports an increase of 17.46 ± 2.93, highlighting its
function in eosinophil recruitment. Collectively, these values illustrate a varied profile of mediator responses, underscoring the
complexity of the immune reaction under investigation. Each mediator's increase reflects distinct biological roles and
interactions within the immune system, providing insights into potential therapeutic targets.
Mediator  Increase over control (pg/mL)
Eotaxin  17.46 ± 2.93
